name: claude-code-walkthrough
description: A friendly guided tour of Claude Code for first-time users

walkthroughs.title: Welcome to Claude Code
walkthroughs.description: Your creative coding journey begins here. Let's explore your new toolkit together.

steps.title: Meet Your Workspace
steps.description:
This is VS Code — your creative canvas. On the left is the **Explorer** where your files live. At the bottom is the **Terminal** where Claude Code runs.

[Open the Explorer](command:workbench.view.explorer)

steps.title: Open a Project Folder
steps.description:
Claude Code works best when you open a folder — it can then see your files and help you build.

Click below to choose a folder, or create a new one for your first project.

[Open Folder](command:workbench.action.files.openFolder)

steps.title: Open the Terminal
steps.description:
The terminal is where you'll talk to Claude. It's a text window at the bottom of VS Code.

Press **Ctrl+`** (backtick) or click below to open it.

[Open Terminal](command:workbench.action.terminal.new)

steps.title: Start Claude Code
steps.description:
In the terminal, type **claude** and press Enter.

This wakes up your AI assistant. The first time, you'll sign in with your Anthropic account.

```
claude
```

Once signed in, you can start chatting!

steps.title: Your First Conversation
steps.description:
Try asking Claude something simple:

```
claude "What can you help me build?"
```

Or describe what you want to create:

```
claude "Help me make a simple webpage"
```

Claude will write code, create files, and explain everything along the way.

steps.title: Discover Commands
steps.description:
Claude Code has built-in commands that start with `/`:

- **/help** — See what's available
- **/getting-started** — A guided tutorial
- **/commit** — Save your work to Git

Type `/help` in a Claude conversation to explore.

steps.title: Claude in the Sidebar
steps.description:
You also have the **Claude extension** in your sidebar (look for the Claude icon on the left).

This lets you chat with Claude right inside VS Code without using the terminal.

[Open Claude Panel](command:claude-code.focus)

steps.title: What Will You Create?
steps.description:
You're ready! Here are some ideas to try:

- **"Create a todo list app"**
- **"Help me organize my photos folder"**
- **"Explain how this code works"** (select some code first)
- **"Write a Python script that..."**

Claude learns from your conversation. The more context you give, the better it helps.

[Start Creating](command:workbench.action.terminal.new)

commands.title: Claude Code: Open Getting Started Guide
